items.text_raw: Not just lately. Anyone reading JT for the past several years can see these things have skyrocketed Then again, anybody following the news in Japan for the past several decades may end up thinking the same about the last few of them. And for historians the "lately" where the "sick things" skyrocket may be the past couple of centuries. The point is that this impression is much more likely to be just availability bias, things fresh in our memories seems to be more likely or frequent.
items.translation_ja: 最近だけの話じゃない。ここ数年JTを読んでいる人なら、こういう事件が急増しているのが分かる。とはいえ、何十年も日本のニュースを追ってきた人なら、直近の数年についても同じように感じるかもしれない。歴史家にとっては、「最近」で「ひどいことが」急増している時期は、過去数百年のことかもしれない。要するに、この印象は可用性バイアス——記憶に新しい出来事ほど、起きやすく頻繁だと感じる——のせいである可能性がずっと高い、ということだ。
notes_ja: 「Then again」は「とはいえ／一方で」と話題をひっくり返す口語的な接続。
「skyrocketed」のあと句点がなく、最後はカンマで文をつないでいる（書き言葉としてはやや乱れている）。
items.standard_en: Not just lately. Anyone reading JT for the past several years can see that these things have skyrocketed. Then again, anybody following the news in Japan for the past several decades may end up thinking the same about the last few years. And for historians, the "lately" when "sick things" skyrocket may be the past couple of centuries. The point is that this impression is much more likely to be just availability bias: things fresh in our memories seem more likely or more frequent.
items.author: virusrex

items.text_raw: The 75-year-old man attacked a passenger seated nearby because of "noise created by reading a newspaper"...
items.translation_ja: 75歳の男性が、近くに座っていた乗客を、「新聞を読むときの音」が原因で襲った……
notes_ja: 末尾の「...」は呆れや言い淀み——「まさかそんな理由で」という含み。
引用符内は記事の言い回しをそのまま繰り返している。
items.author: 3RENSHO

items.text_raw: Mickelicious Sounds like you're not the person with time on his hands. Tread softly if you value it. I have no idea what that means. And, I have nothing but time on my hands, what with being retired and all. kurisupisu My advice is to ignore the complainer. The police in Japan won’t be of much help as what can they do to your neighbor without an actionable crime? The dude is a million years old. (I'm half a million, myself.) I'm not worried about any danger whatsoever. But, I will not tolerate any of his annoying whining.
items.translation_ja: Mickelicious 暇つぶしの時間がある人じゃなさそうだね。大事にするなら、慎重に行動した方がいいよ。それが何の意味かさっぱり分からない。それに、俺は退職してるんだから、暇はいっぱいあるよ。kurisupisu 文句を言う人は無視するのがいいと思う。日本の警察はあまり役に立たないだろう。立件できる犯罪がなければ、隣人に何ができる？あいつはめちゃくちゃ年を取ってる。（俺だって半分くらいはそうだけど。）危険性なんてまったく心配してない。でも、あいつのうっとうしい文句だけは我慢しない。
notes_ja: 「time on his hands」＝暇がある、という慣用表現。「Tread softly if you value it」は相手の言葉をそのまま返す皮肉っぽい言い方。
「what with being retired and all」は口語の「退職してるっていうのもあって／なんてね」という付け足し。
items.standard_en: Mickelicious, it sounds like you're not the person with time on your hands. Tread softly if you value it. I have no idea what that means. And I have nothing but time on my hands, what with being retired and all. kurisupisu, my advice is to ignore the complainer. The police in Japan won't be of much help, as what can they do to your neighbor without an actionable crime? The guy is extremely old. (I'm half a million years old myself.) I'm not worried about any danger whatsoever. But I will not tolerate any of his annoying whining.

marked_terms.note_ja: 「男／あいつ」のくだけた呼び方。フォーマルな場では避ける。

items.author: garypen

items.text_raw: My advice is to ignore the complainer. Any interaction will prompt escalation and your wife is likely to become a possible target. The police in Japan won’t be of much help as what can they do to your neighbor without an actionable crime? I would be very wary. That's called living on your knees.
items.translation_ja: 文句を言う人は無視するのがいい。何か関わればエスカレートするし、奥さんが標的になりかねない。日本の警察はあまり役に立たないだろう。立件できる犯罪がなければ、隣人に何ができる？すごく警戒した方がいい。それは膝をついて生きることだ。
notes_ja: 「living on your knees」は屈辱的に従う生き方——ここでは警察に頼れず我慢する状況への強い批判。
「as what can they do」は口語的な理由のつなぎ（because / since の代わり）。
items.standard_en: My advice is to ignore the complainer. Any interaction will prompt escalation, and your wife is likely to become a possible target. The police in Japan won't be of much help, as what can they do to your neighbor without an actionable crime? I would be very wary. That's called living on your knees.
items.author: yakyak

items.text_raw: section321 My Japanese girlfriend theorized that many of these oji were once managers of some kind and they are now upset that no one kisses up to them on a daily basis. Definitely! My wife said the same thing about the old man who lives next door to the property where we are about to build a new home. He used to be an exec at some big insurance/financial company (Sumitomo?) He started complaining when we were having the old house demolished, and hasn't stopped bothering our builder's agent since then. He even refused a gift from us, saying if he accepted he wouldn't be able to complain anymore! I can't wait until the first time he complains directly to us after we move in, so I can tell him to go blank himself. (But, in a nice way, of course.)
items.translation_ja: section321 うちの日本人の彼女は、こういうおじさんの多くは昔何かの管理職で、今は毎日誰にも媚びられないことに腹を立てているんじゃないか、と推測した。まさにその通り！妻も、これから新しい家を建てる土地の隣に住む老人のことで同じことを言っていた。彼は大手の保険／金融会社（住友？）の役員だったらしい。古い家を解体しているときから文句を言い始めて、それ以来建築会社の担当者にしつこく迷惑をかけ続けている。うちからの贈り物まで断って、「受け取ったらもう文句が言えなくなる」と言った！引っ越してから直接文句を言ってきたときが待ち遠しい。あいつにくたばれと言ってやれるから。（もちろん、上品にね。）
notes_ja: 「oji」は日本語の「おじさん」を英語圏コメント欄で使うくだけた呼び方。
「go blank himself」は「go fuck yourself」の伏せ字。括弧内は皮肉で、実際は攻撃的な言い方をするつもりという含み。
items.standard_en: section321, my Japanese girlfriend theorized that many of these old men were once managers of some kind and are now upset that no one kisses up to them on a daily basis. Definitely! My wife said the same thing about the old man who lives next door to the property where we are about to build a new home. He used to be an executive at some big insurance/financial company (Sumitomo?). He started complaining when we were having the old house demolished, and hasn't stopped bothering our builder's agent since then. He even refused a gift from us, saying that if he accepted it, he wouldn't be able to complain anymore! I can't wait until the first time he complains directly to us after we move in, so I can tell him to go fuck himself. (But, in a nice way, of course.)

marked_terms.note_ja: 「おじさん」の略。年配男性を指すくだけた・やや蔑むニュアンスのスラング。
媚びへつらう、というくだけた表現。
「go fuck yourself（くたばれ／失せろ）」の伏せ字。非常に攻撃的で失礼。丁寧な場では絶対に使わない。

items.author: garypen
